heading: Transportation of official records and papers to House Member’s district
label: 5346 Transportation of official records and papers to House Member’s district

children.heading: Payment of reasonable expenses from applicable accounts of House; rules and regulations
children.label: a Payment of reasonable expenses from applicable accounts of House; rules and regulations
children.text: Effective , notwithstanding any provision of law and until otherwise provided by law, the applicable accounts of the House shall be available to pay the reasonable expenses of sending or transporting the official records and papers of any Member of the House of Representatives from the District of Columbia to any location designated by such Member in the district represented by the Member.August 16, 1978The Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives is authorized and directed to provide for the most economical means of sending or transporting such documents to insure the orderly and timely delivery to the specified location. The Committee on House Oversight shall have the authority to issue rules and regulations to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: “Member of the House of Representatives” and “official records and papers” defined
children.label: b “Member of the House of Representatives” and “official records and papers” defined

children.text: the term “Member of the House of Representatives” means a Representative in, or a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to, the Congress; and
the term “official records and papers” means books, records, papers, and official files which could be sent as franked mail.
